regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather is expected to deteriorate over the next 3 days, with increasing rain chances and winds. Cruise passengers and yacht captains visiting ports such as San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten can expect overcast conditions and 40-70% rain chances, with east winds 13-15kt. Fishing captains should exercise caution when navigating waters around St. Kitts and Antigua, as seas may become rough due to increasing winds, and isolated thunderstorms may develop.
regions.western-caribbean: Partly cloudy conditions with east to southeast winds at 8-11 knots are expected to prevail in the Western Caribbean over the next three days, with no significant precipitation forecasted for ports such as Cozumel, Costa Maya, and Grand Cayman. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can anticipate smooth sailing conditions, while fishing captains should be prepared for gentle to moderate winds. In Roatan and Belize City, similar conditions are expected, with partly cloudy skies and light winds, making for ideal anchorage conditions.
regions.bahamas: Current conditions in the Bahamas are favorable with clear skies and light winds, making for ideal anchorage conditions in ports such as Nassau and Freeport. Over the next three days, expect increasing cloud cover and winds, with a slight chance of rain, particularly in the Berry Islands and Turks & Caicos. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should prepare for potential drizzle on day three, while fishing captains should be aware of the increasing winds and potential for choppy seas, especially around Bimini.
regions.southern-caribbean: Easterly winds at 17-20 knots will prevail across the Southern Caribbean region, including Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, over the next three days. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect overcast conditions with increasing chances of rain, reaching 23% by day three. Small Craft Advisory conditions are likely due to sustained winds and potential seas, so fishing captains and mariners should exercise caution when navigating the waters around Cartagena and other ports. Anchorage conditions may become unsettled, particularly in exposed areas, so vessels should be prepared for potential wind and wave impacts.
regions.central-caribbean: Expect partly cloudy conditions with moderate easterly winds at 12 knots for the next three days across the Central Caribbean region, including ports such as Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can anticipate calm seas with wave heights around 2-4 feet, making for smooth navigation. Fishing captains should be aware of the increasing rain chance, up to 20% by Day 2, and plan accordingly to avoid potential thunderstorms. No tropical cyclone activity is forecast for the region at this time, with only typical tropical moisture present.
regions.lesser-antilles: Northeasterly winds at 19 knots will persist across the Lesser Antilles region for the next three days, with partly cloudy to overcast skies and rain chances ranging from 8 to 25 percent. Cruise passengers and yacht captains can expect moderate seas and gusty conditions, particularly when approaching ports such as St. Maarten and St. Kitts, where anchorage conditions may be rough. Fishing captains should be aware of the potential for strong winds and reduced visibility in squalls, and plan their routes accordingly, especially near the islands of Dominica and Grenada. A Small Craft Advisory may be in effect for some areas, and mariners should exercise caution when navigating through the region.
regions.los-angeles: Current conditions in the Los Angeles region are favorable with clear skies and light winds at 9 knots. For the next three days, foggy conditions are expected to move in, particularly affecting ports such as Long Beach and Marina del Rey, with winds ranging from 7 to 14 knots. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should exercise caution when navigating through foggy areas, especially when approaching or departing from Catalina Island. Fishing captains should be prepared for changing wind conditions and reduced visibility, and consider adjusting their fishing plans accordingly.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region is currently experiencing clear skies with a temperature of 61F and west winds at 7kt. For the next 3 days, expect changing conditions with a chance of drizzle on Day 1, overcast skies on Day 2, and partly cloudy skies on Day 3. Cruise passengers and yacht captains should prepare for increased winds on Day 1, with west winds at 12kt, and possible rain showers in the Ensenada and Punta Banda areas. Fishing captains should exercise caution when navigating near Islas Todos Santos due to potential rough seas.
